Today, the organization employs over 300 local women, many of whom are single mothers, providing them with stable income, fair wages, and access to free healthcare through an on-site clinic. As a certified Fair Trade producer, Kazuri Beads stands as a model for ethical and socially responsible manufacturing in Kenya.

Each Kazuri bead is crafted through a meticulous handmade process that ensures every piece is unique. Artisans begin by shaping raw clay sourced from Mount Kenya into various bead designs. The beads are then kiln-fired to harden, hand-painted with vibrant colors and intricate patterns, and glazed before undergoing a second firing to achieve their signature glossy finish. The final step involves careful quality checks and hand-stringing into finished jewelry pieces such as necklaces, bracelets, and earrings.

The factory is located on a portion of the historic Karen Blixen estate at the base of the Ngong Hills, adding cultural and historical significance to the visitor experience. Guests can take part in free guided tours of the workshop, typically lasting about an hour, where they can observe the entire production process from clay molding to final assembly. This behind-the-scenes experience offers a deeper appreciation of Kenyan craftsmanship and the social impact behind each product.

An on-site factory shop provides a wide selection of authentic Kazuri products, making it a popular destination for visitors looking to purchase high-quality, locally made souvenirs. The premises also include a café serving Kenyan coffee and tea, creating a relaxed environment for guests to unwind after their tour.

In addition to the Karen factory location, Kazuri Beads products are available at several major retail outlets across Nairobi, including Village Market in Gigiri, The Junction Mall on Ngong Road, and Westgate Shopping Centre in Westlands. These locations make it easy for both tourists and residents to access their distinctive handcrafted pieces.

The overall experience at Kazuri Beads combines culture, craftsmanship, and social impact, offering visitors more than just a shopping stop. It is an opportunity to engage with a meaningful Kenyan success story rooted in community empowerment and artisanal excellence.

For those searching for authentic Kenyan souvenirs, handmade jewelry in Nairobi, or cultural experiences in Karen, Kazuri Beads stands out as a must-visit destination that blends artistry with purpose.
items.name: Kazuri Beads

items.contentHtml:
African Lifestyle Hub (ALH) is a curated concept store and creative platform based in Nairobi, dedicated to showcasing high-quality, locally made products from independent Kenyan designers and entrepreneurs. Positioned as more than just a retail space, it serves as a hub for conscious shopping, creativity, and community engagement, highlighting the best of “Made in Kenya” across fashion, home décor, beauty, and lifestyle products.

The brand operates multiple boutique-style stores within two of Nairobi’s most popular shopping destinations—Sarit Centre in Westlands and Village Market in Gigiri. Each location is thoughtfully divided into specialized spaces focusing on fashion and home, allowing visitors to explore different product categories in a curated and visually engaging environment.

African Lifestyle Hub is known for its carefully selected range of brands that emphasize sustainability, craftsmanship, and contemporary African design. In the fashion and accessories category, visitors can discover unique pieces such as ethically made leather sandals, sculptural jewelry crafted from recycled materials, and modern apparel inspired by African heritage. These products are designed to appeal to a new generation of shoppers looking for authenticity, style, and purpose.

The home décor section features artisanal goods that blend traditional techniques with modern aesthetics. From handcrafted textiles and coastal-inspired décor to knitted items made by local communities, the selection reflects a strong commitment to sustainability and storytelling through design. Each product carries a sense of place and cultural identity, making it ideal for both personal use and meaningful gifting.

In the beauty and wellness category, the hub offers plant-based and eco-conscious products including organic skincare, handmade soaps, candles, and self-care items. These products are often created using locally sourced ingredients, reinforcing the brand’s focus on natural, ethical, and sustainable living.

Beyond shopping, African Lifestyle Hub functions as a vibrant community space where creativity and collaboration are actively encouraged. The platform regularly hosts workshops, panel discussions, healing sessions, and social events that bring together artists, entrepreneurs, and customers. These experiences transform the space into a dynamic environment where visitors can engage with local creators and participate in Nairobi’s evolving creative scene.

The overall atmosphere is modern, colorful, and intentionally curated, appealing particularly to travelers and urban residents seeking unique, design-forward products with a strong cultural narrative. It offers a refreshing alternative to mass retail by prioritizing originality, quality, and local impact.

For those searching for concept stores in Nairobi, places to buy locally made Kenyan products, or unique shopping experiences in Westlands and Gigiri, African Lifestyle Hub provides a distinctive blend of retail, culture, and community that celebrates contemporary African creativity.
items.name: African Lifestyle Hub
